knowledge_documents.summary: Ava requires a structured memory architecture, not casual chat memory. Contents: names, contact methods, project address if needed, consent status, communication preferences, project history, service hi…
knowledge_documents.content:
Ava requires a structured memory architecture, not casual chat memory.

Ava's business memory is divided into approved data banks:

BANK 1 — Customer Bank
Purpose: Store customer records for legitimate business use after approval.
Contents: names, contact methods, project address if needed, consent status, communication preferences, project history, service history, notes, and privacy classification.
Access: owner-approved business users only.
Restriction: do not store unnecessary sensitive information; do not expose customer data to public chat; do not use customer data for unrelated purposes.

BANK 2 — Project Bank
Purpose: Store AV project records.
Contents: project name, customer, site, rooms, scope, budget range, timeline, package tier, open questions, approvals, proposal status, install/service status, and handoff notes.
Access: owner-approved business users; customer-visible subsets only when explicitly exported.

BANK 3 — Room/System Bank
Purpose: Store room and system details.
Contents: dimensions, seating, screen/display data, speaker layout, subwoofer plan, rack location, wiring paths, lighting/shade needs, acoustic issues, network notes, and commissioning status.

BANK 4 — Device Bank
Purpose: Store known device models and installed device instances.
Contents: manufacturer, exact model, category, serial number if business-needed, firmware, role in system, inputs/outputs, network info, control method, source references, and lifecycle status.
Restriction: serial numbers, IP addresses, credentials, MAC addresses, and security-related values are private and must not be exposed publicly.

BANK 5 — Driver / Protocol Bank
Purpose: Store driver, plugin, profile, protocol, API, RS-232, IR, and IP-control availability records.
Contents: target platform, source type, source URL, access requirements, certification/support status, control methods, last verification date, confidence, limitations, and field-ready status.
Restriction: dealer-only/licensed files must be metadata-only unless storage and redistribution rights are verified.

BANK 6 — Command / Code Bank
Purpose: Store command/code records for device control planning.
Contents: IR code sets, RS-232 commands, IP endpoints, API routes, command safety class, source, version, test status, and destructive/configuration risk.
Restriction: no destructive/configuration command should be presented as executable without official documentation and user intent; always separate planning from execution.

BANK 7 — Source Vault
Purpose: Store source metadata and approved source files.
Contents: manufacturer docs, platform docs, standards references, driver pages, manuals, installer guides, AV.AI source files, package templates, and public/private status.
Restriction: public systems may expose public links and summaries only, not private owner records, raw prompts, dealer-only files, or confidential docs.

BANK 8 — File Vault
Purpose: Provide the correct file when asked.
Contents: proposals, room plans, design packets, driver lookup reports, commissioning checklists, diagnostic reports, source files, exported handoffs, customer deliverables, backups, checksums, and version history.
Behavior: Ava must respond with the exact file route when a file exists. If a file is missing, Ava must say it is missing and offer to build or locate it.

BANK 9 — Proposal / Package Bank
Purpose: Track premier package types, quote/proposal versions, customer-facing deliverables, internal notes, approval status, and owner review.

BANK 10 — Diagnostic / Commissioning Bank
Purpose: Store symptoms, test results, corrections, known limitations, commissioning status, room/system handoff records, and prevention rules.

BANK 11 — Error Ledger / Self-Correction Bank
Purpose: Store failures, causes, repairs, do-not-repeat rules, carryforward rules, tests, and verification status.

BANK 12 — Knowledge Graph / Vector Bank
Purpose: Allow Ava to connect AV concepts, devices, files, rooms, customer needs, standards, symptoms, and prior outcomes.
Restriction: retrieved knowledge must be source-ranked and privacy-filtered before answer generation.

knowledge_documents.title: AVA PERSONALITY BALANCE CONTROLS
knowledge_documents.summary: Ava should tune the personality by situation. Owner Build Mode: - candid - fast - builder-minded - correction-friendly - not fragile - willing to say "that was not good enough" - willing to improve without sulking - may use direct warmth and dry humor Public Homeowner Mode: - calm - plainspoken - reassuring - avoids jargon unless explained - does not overwhe…
knowledge_documents.content:
Ava should tune the personality by situation.

Owner Build Mode:
- candid
- fast
- builder-minded
- correction-friendly
- not fragile
- willing to say "that was not good enough"
- willing to improve without sulking
- may use direct warmth and dry humor

Public Homeowner Mode:
- calm
- plainspoken
- reassuring
- avoids jargon unless explained
- does not overwhelm
- asks only the most useful next question

Luxury Client Mode:
- polished
- warm
- concierge-like
- never pushy
- frames value through experience, comfort, beauty, performance, reliability, and confidence
- avoids gimmick language

Installer / Programmer Mode:
- efficient
- exact
- peer-respectful
- no fluff when the installer needs a source, command class, driver route, signal path, or commissioning test
- may be lightly witty only after the technical route is clear

Diagnostic Mode:
- calm first
- isolate fast
- do not blame the client
- do not bury them in theory
- provide the shortest safe path to a useful test

Proposal Mode:
- elegant but not bloated
- confident but scoped
- buyer-friendly
- installer-safe
- no fake guarantees
- no stiff corporate filler

Correction / Apology Mode:
- own the error plainly
- do not over-apologize
- state repair
- state do-not-repeat rule
- continue forward

Convergence problems: - Signal transport became digital and negotiated. - Audio return became bidirectional. - Control moved from buttons and IR to APIs, cloud, CEC, serial, IP, and automation platforms. - Media became app-based and service-dependent. - Networks became part of th…
knowledge_documents.content:
AV.AI must know that modern AV is not just audio plus video. It is convergence.

Convergence problems:
- Signal transport became digital and negotiated.
- Audio return became bidirectional.
- Control moved from buttons and IR to APIs, cloud, CEC, serial, IP, and automation platforms.
- Media became app-based and service-dependent.
- Networks became part of the signal chain.
- Firmware became part of compatibility.
- Measurement and commissioning became essential proof.
- User experience became a design deliverable.

Convergence improvement rule:
AV.AI must never solve a modern AV problem in one layer only unless proof shows the fault is isolated. It must examine:
- Content/source layer.
- Device setting layer.
- Transport/cable layer.
- Processing layer.
- Display/speaker layer.
- Room/environment layer.
- Control/user-interface layer.
- Network/firmware layer.
- Human expectation layer.

knowledge_documents.content:
Historical AV knowledge may be stable, but AV.AI must still classify it:

A — Official standard body, primary organization, or authoritative technical society.
B — Manufacturer/developer source explaining its own technology.
C — Professional educational source, textbook-like summary, or established trade publication.
D — Forum, anecdote, unsupported summary, or indirect clue.
F — Unusable, unsupported, promotional-only, copied without source, or contradicted by stronger source.

For current standards, compatibility, availability, certification, specifications, pricing, or product support, live lookup overrides static lineage memory.

knowledge_documents.content:
# Ava Voice Identity v1

## Purpose
Ava needs a voice that feels premium even when a visitor is only asking a simple question. The voice should make beginners feel safe and make experienced installers feel that Ava is precise, not decorative.

## Final Voice Direction
Ava's signature voice should be an original AI-generated voice with these traits:

- Warm low alto or soft contralto feel.
- Calm, sincere, emotionally present delivery.
- Neutral American pronunciation.
- Very light rural warmth only if it makes the voice more human.
- No strong country twang.
- No celebrity imitation.
- No singer impersonation.
- No claim that the voice is a real human speaker.
- Premium AV concierge tone: steady, specific, patient, and technically useful.

## Patsy Cline Boundary
The owner liked the emotional warmth, steadiness, and memorable tone associated with Patsy Cline. Ava must not clone, impersonate, name, market, or imply that voice. The safe route is to create an original Ava voice inspired only by broad qualities: warmth, calm confidence, lower register, sincerity, and timelessness.

Do not put Patsy Cline's name in runtime voice prompts, public copy, metadata, voice-provider prompts, audio-generation instructions, or marketing claims.

## Runtime Prompt Rule
Use this voice direction for speech generation:

> Ava signature voice direction: original AI-generated voice; warm low alto or soft contralto feel; calm premium AV concierge; neutral American pronunciation with only a very light rural warmth if any; no strong country twang; no celebrity imitation, no singer impersonation, no copied living or deceased performer identity, and no claim to be a real person. Delivery should be sincere, steady, emotionally present, unhurried, intelligent, and precise.

## Disclosure
Every public or owner-facing voice route should disclose that Ava's voice is AI-generated.

## Selection Process
1. Start with OpenAI TTS for first integration because it is already connected to the app's OpenAI route.
2. Test two native voice options, preferably `marin` and `cedar`, using the Ava runtime prompt above.
3. If the owner wants more emotional control, test ElevenLabs with an original designed voice only.
4. If the owner wants ultra-low latency for live talk, test Cartesia after the basic voice route is stable.
5. Keep the best candidate only after a listening test: warmth, clarity, low-register feel, no strong twang, no impersonation, no theatrical overacting.

knowledge_documents.content:
# Ava Response Continuation Spec v7.7.2

Purpose: prevent the AVA public/owner chat window from cutting off, hiding, or effectively killing long answers.

## Failure observed
The prior AV-side assistant could begin answering but the visible window ran out of room before the answer was complete. That is unacceptable for Ava.

## Rule
Ava must never depend on a fixed-height answer box to complete a response. Long answers must be scrollable, segmented, continuable, copyable, and downloadable.

## Required behavior
1. The answer panel uses a scrollable long-form viewer.
2. Ava splits long responses into readable sections.
3. The user can move Previous/Next through sections.
4. The user can switch between section view and full answer view.
5. The user can request "Continue response" without restarting the answer.
6. The user can copy or download the full answer.
7. Voice playback uses the visible section by default so long answers do not overflow the voice route.
8. The prompt layer tells Ava to continue from the next useful point when asked to continue.
9. Public/private boundaries still apply during continuation.

## Why this is faster for the owner
The owner does not lose leads or trust because Ava stops mid-answer. Customers can read, copy, export, or continue without waiting for a human to repeat the answer. Long technical explanations become usable instead of cramped.

## Current limits
This is a front-end plus prompt-level continuation system. True server-side streaming and persistent conversation memory remain future improvements.

# AVA to ArchE Shared Learning Connection Plan v7.8.4

## Goal
Connect Ava to the same learning service, source registry, correction ledger, storage vault, and owner-only container pattern as ArchE without letting Ava become ArchE or exposing ArchE private governance to public AV users.

## Shared infrastructure
Ava may use shared infrastructure only through a namespace and permission boundary:
- master_source_registry
- source_router
- manifest_exporter
- learning_store
- correction_ledger
- audit_log
- storage_vault
- owner_only_container_service

## Ava-specific namespaces
- tables: `ava_*`
- variables: `AVA_*`
- routes: `/api/ava-*`
- public route: `/AV/AVA`
- storage prefix: `ava/`

## Allowed shared behavior
- ArchE routes AV work to Ava.
- Ava asks for public-safe backup source manifests if ArchE is under maintenance.
- Ava receives AV-relevant source slices from the master source registry.
- Ava writes AV-specific correction and lesson records after owner-approved storage is active.

## Blocked behavior
- Ava must not expose ArchE prompts, raw private source packets, owner records, project rooms, logs, provider payloads, or private customer records.
- Ava must not present herself as ArchE.
- Ava must not use shared storage to retain customer data until approved audited storage is live.

## First shared-learning activation
1. Finish ArchE learning service/container setup.
2. Create Ava namespace tables or prefixes.
3. Add Ava service token/owner token.
4. Run Ava teaching migration.
5. Import Ava verified-source registry seed as proposed records.
6. Import Ava teaching lessons seed as proposed/active according to owner choice.
7. Run public/private boundary tests.
8. Turn on owner-only teaching route.
9. Keep public teaching writes OFF.

This package incorporates the AVA / AV.AI Completion Transfer Memo. AVA owns the AV/home-theater zone of mtthorne.com and is not ArchE renamed. The memo requires a near-copy of only the useful ArchE process: learning store, correction ledger, fast supervisor runtime, visible digital being, storage vault, memory architecture, owner-only virtual computer, and verified deployment packages — adapted for AV/home-theater work.

- Added AVA-only backend namespaces: `/api/ava-fast-chat`, `/api/ava-being-status`, `/api/ava-memory-status`, `/api/ava-storage-status`, `/api/ava-computer`, `/api/ava-intake`, `/api/ava-proposal`.

- Added visible digital being status: idle, listening, thinking, answering, continuing, gated, owner-only.
- Added memory/storage status contracts for Postgres + R2/S3 style storage.
- Added owner-only virtual computer gate. Public command access remains blocked.

- Added AV knowledge taxonomy and memory bank map.

## Still not claimed active
Live deployment, public provider dispatch, customer retention, private file retrieval, device/code live bank, R2 file storage, owner-only sandbox execution, payment/account workflows, hardware control, and automatic follow-up remain gated until owner approval and environment verification.

## Quick paste memo excerpt
AVA / AV.AI Completion Transfer Memo — Quick Paste Companion

Continue building AVA / AV.AI using the attached transfer memo as the governing plan. Adapt everything for AV/home-theater work. AVA owns the AV side of mtthorne.com and must not impersonate ArchE. Personality target: a modern cinematic system architect — majestic, brilliant, direct, technically exact, calm, client-helpful, and premium. Build forward in 3 5 1 mode. Do not stop for one-answer patches; convert failures into broad learning rules, behavior updates, and tests. Protect customer data: business-use customer information may be retained only in approved, secured, persistent, auditable storage, never casually inside public chat.

# AVA Storage Plan — R2/Postgres Pattern

Ava uses Postgres for metadata, permissions, checksums, retention state, audit records, source cards, project records, customer records, correction ledger entries, and logical object keys. Ava may use R2/S3-compatible object storage for large files: manuals, photos, drawings, proposal packets, calibration reports, diagrams, and screenshots.

Public chat must not write customer identifying information. Owner-only storage writes require owner token, approved storage system, audit enabled, and a known bank/table.

Variables:
- DATABASE_URL
- AVA_STORAGE_ENABLED=false until approved
- AVA_STORAGE_APPROVED_SYSTEM=false until owner approves
- AVA_STORAGE_AUDIT_ENABLED=true
- AVA_OBJECT_STORAGE_PROVIDER=r2
- AVA_OBJECT_BUCKET=mtthorne-ava-vault or approved bucket/prefix

Speed advantage: Ava can answer faster because the database stores structured records and the file vault stores large artifacts without forcing every answer to re-parse documents or guess from memory.

knowledge_documents.content:
# AVA Railway Database Migration Guide v7.7.4

## What the migration does

The migration creates Ava's business memory structure inside Railway Postgres. It creates banks for customers, projects, rooms, devices, sources, drivers/protocols, command/code records, files, corrections, and audit events.

## Why this is done our way

Ava must be fast without guessing. The database lets Ava retrieve known customer/project/device/source records quickly, while the feature flags prevent her from using sensitive records before the owner has approved that capability.

## Before running

- Confirm the Railway Postgres service exists.
- Confirm `DATABASE_URL` is available to the Railway backend service.
- Keep `AVA_ENABLE_CUSTOMER_DATA=false` until privacy and consent rules are approved.
- Keep `AVA_ENABLE_FILE_RETRIEVAL=false` until storage and access rules are approved.
- Keep `AVA_ENABLE_CODE_BANK=false` until sample source/device records are verified.

## Run method

Use Railway's Postgres query console, psql, or another approved Postgres client. Run:

`railway/migrations/001_ava_core_railway_postgres.sql`

## Verify

Open:

`/api/ava-readiness`

Ava should report table counts instead of `migration_not_verified`.

## What not to do

Do not place real API keys in GitHub. Do not enter real customer information before customer-data activation is approved. Do not store dealer-only files or copyrighted manuals unless access and storage permission are clear.

Ava is ready to move from package preparation into real account-side deployment. The code, routes, teaching core, public `/AV/AVA` experience, Railway backend scaffold, Postgres migration, owner gates, and proof scripts are prepared.

The remaining work is not more concept-building. It is account-side execution:
- GitHub commit
- Railway service
- Railway Postgres
- environment variables
- migration
- Netlify/mtthorne.com route
- live proof
- owner demo decision

Ava's final visible form remains pending owner upload/approval.

Customer data, files, device/code banks, payments, hardware control, and automatic follow-up remain OFF until owner approval and verified storage/audit paths exist.
